framework.name: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164, Subpart C)
framework.note: A convenience index, NOT a compliance claim or legal advice. These are the HIPAA Security Rule safeguards a runtime audit record of an AI system handling ePHI is most relevant to. A tag means a record may be relevant to that safeguard for review; it does not assert the safeguard is met. IMPORTANT: an audit trail of a system that handles ePHI is itself ePHI and must be protected accordingly (Ballast redacts by default, but the trail is in scope). Ballast is not a full HIPAA solution. Revise for your own context.

controls.title: Audit controls
controls.summary: Implement mechanisms that record and examine activity in systems that contain or use ePHI.
controls.relevance: Ballast is an audit-control mechanism: it records and lets you examine the AI system's activity.

controls.title: Information system activity review
controls.summary: Regularly review records of information system activity such as audit logs and incident reports.
controls.relevance: Ballast's trail is the record of activity to be reviewed; ballast log and summary support that review.

controls.title: Integrity
controls.summary: Protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction, with a mechanism to corroborate it has not been altered.
controls.relevance: Ballast's hash-chained, optionally sealed trail is relevant to detecting improper alteration and to integrity review.

controls.title: Security incident procedures (response and reporting)
controls.summary: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents and document them and their outcomes.
controls.relevance: Ballast flags and records incidents (dangerous or blocked actions) with a tamper-evident, timestamped trail relevant to incident identification and documentation.

controls.title: Minimum necessary
controls.summary: Limit PHI to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose.
controls.relevance: Ballast redacts PII and PHI from stored records by default, relevant to keeping the audit trail to the minimum necessary.
